citations.narrative:
In June 2025, the facility misdosed 30 guinea pigs, which resulted in the study needing to be repeated. The facility
intended for 30 animals to be dosed with test article #1 and a second group of 30 animals to be dosed with test article #2.
The test article identification numbers on the vials were very similar to each other, so group #1 received test article #2
instead of test article #1. The error was quickly identified and the second group of 30 guinea pigs were then dosed with
test article #1. The study continued without incident and the animals were handled per protocol. However, after thorough
review, the study director, quality assurance team, and IACUC determined that the study would need to be repeated to
meet regulatory documentation requirements. The studies were repeated with an additional 60 guinea pigs.
Due to this misdosing and unclear documentation, the facility was required to repeat the studies using an additional 60
guinea pigs that would not have been needed for these studies. Mishandling of study animals and test articles can have
negative impacts to the animals, such as animal loss or unnecessary animal use.
Handling of all animals, including the administration of test articles to study animals, shall be done as expeditiously and
carefully as possible.
Correct by March 25, 2026. The facility implemented corrective actions prior to inspection.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.38(f)(1) 
   
Miscellaneous. 
In June 2025, the facility misdosed 30 guinea pigs, which resulted in the study needing to be repeated. The facility 
intended for 30 animals to be dosed with test article #1 and a second group of 30 animals to be dosed with test article #2. 
The test article identification numbers on the vials were very similar to each other, so group #1 received test article #2 
instead of test article #1. The error was quickly identified and the second group of 30 guinea pigs were then dosed with 
test article #1. The study continued without incident and the animals were handled per protocol. However, after thorough 
review, the study director, quality assurance team, and IACUC determined that the study would need to be repeated to 
meet regulatory documentation requirements. The studies were repeated with an additional 60 guinea pigs.  
 
Due to this misdosing and unclear documentation, the facility was required to repeat the studies using an additional 60 
guinea pigs that would not have been needed for these studies. Mishandling of study animals and test articles can have 
negative impacts to the animals, such as animal loss or unnecessary animal use.  
 
Handling of all animals, including the administration of test articles to study animals, shall be done as expeditiously and 
carefully as possible.  
 
Correct by March 25, 2026. The facility implemented corrective actions prior to inspection. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
